rationale.R1.1: The contribution claim 'strictly dominant deployment strategy' is a strong thesis assertion anchoring the entire section; the framing premise 'uniformly improved execution accuracy' is the load-bearing predicate that must be verified before this claim can stand.
rationale.R1.2: Load-bearing: the section asserts 'uniformly improved execution accuracy' and concludes 'strictly dominant', yet the presented numbers directly falsify uniform improvement — RouteSQL underperforms the baseline on WikiSQL (89.1% vs 89.6%, −0.5 pp) and SParC question-match (64.2% vs 65.1%, −0.9 pp). The inferential chain from evidence to conclusion is internally broken.
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing: two of five benchmarks (WikiSQL, SParC) yield numbers in the artifact itself that contradict the 'uniformly improved' claim; KaggleDBQA reports no baseline at all, leaving that data point's contribution to the efficiency-accuracy narrative unverifiable. Multiple evidence-claim misalignments exist within this single section.
rationale.R1.7: The DAIL-SQL/GPT-4 baseline is the sole comparison point for all five benchmarks but is referenced without a citation; the choice of this specific baseline, and whether it represents the strongest available published result on each suite, requires scholarly grounding that is currently absent.
